items.title: Watch the moment Artemis II blasts into space
items.summary: After delays and technical issues, the first crewed Moon mission in 50 years finally took off from Florida and is now in Earth's orbit.

items.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
items.summary: There were some glaring omissions in the president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

items.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
items.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

items.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
items.summary: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

items.title: Utah teen identified as victim of serial killer Ted Bundy
items.summary: New DNA testing has solved the 51-year-old case of murdered teenager Laura Ann Aime, according to investigators.

items.title: US Supreme Court appears sceptical of Trump plan to limit birthright citizenship
items.summary: Trump attended the arguments in person, a rare move by a sitting president.

items.title: Mass robotaxi malfunction halts traffic in Chinese city
items.summary: Baidu has not responded to a request for comment about the outage, which affected at least 100 cars.

items.title: Families cram into Greek court for trial into deadliest train crash
items.summary: Fifty-seven people were killed when two trains collided in 2023 in the worst rail accident in Greek history, with 36 accused in a trial expected to last years.

items.title: Devastated Italians reckon with 'third apocalypse' of World Cup failure
items.summary: Azzurri fans and management alike are pondering what went wrong after Italy fail to qualify for third World Cup in a row.

items.title: Explosions at Burundi ammunition depot kill 13 civilians - army
items.summary: Powerful blasts destroyed homes and sparked panic in the city of Bujumbura on Tuesday night.

items.title: Brazil judge blocks Sugarloaf Mountain zipline
items.summary: The project to build a zipline linking the popular Rio tourist spot to Morro da Urca proved controversial.

items.title: South African army arrive in crime hotspots to help tackle gangs
items.summary: The army has been deployed to five of South Africa's nine provinces for a year-long operation.

items.title: Iran war economic shocks will last 'months', says Australia's PM
items.summary: Anthony Albanese used a rare TV address to tell Australians that the "months ahead may not be easy" amid the global effects of the war.

items.title: Megan Thee Stallion taken to hospital during Moulin Rouge performance
items.summary: The rapper says she felt faint while performing in the Broadway show and was later diagnosed with "extreme exhaustion".

items.title: DR Congo declares national holiday after reaching World Cup for first time in 52 years
items.summary: This is just the second time that DR Congo have reached the World Cup finals, sparking celebrations in Kinshasa.

items.title: Divorced couples in Japan can now share custody of their children
items.summary: Japan was, until recently, the only G7 country to not recognise the legal concept of joint custody.

items.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
items.summary: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

items.title: 'My six-year-old has nosebleeds': Chiang Mai air pollution sparks health fears
items.summary: It's peak fire season in the Thai city, which now ranks as one of the world's most polluted.

items.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
items.summary: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

items.title: Thousands lose their jobs in deep cuts at tech giant Oracle
items.summary: It is thought that thousands of people may have lost their jobs at Oracle, one of the world's largest tech companies.

items.title: Judge temporarily halts construction of Trump's White House ballroom
items.summary: The Republican-appointed judge ruled that Trump was a "steward" of the White House, not the owner.

items.title: 'We go for all humanity' - emotional moment as Artemis II blasts off
items.summary: There was giddy euphoria at the Kennedy Space Center after the launch, writes the BBC's Pallab Ghosh.

items.title: What do Trump's latest comments on leaving Nato mean for the alliance?
items.summary: Trump has criticised Nato members for showing a lack of support for US objectives in Iran.

items.title: Peter Magyar, the former Orban ally vying for power in Hungary
items.summary: Magyar represents the biggest threat to Viktor Orban's rule in Hungary since he won the first of four consecutive victories in 2010.

items.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
items.summary: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

items.title: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
items.summary: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

items.title: 'Moonshots' and merch - what it's like to be on Florida's Space Coast ahead of Artemis launch
items.summary: Huge crowds are gathering around the Kennedy Space Centre to see the launch of the historic moon mission.

items.title: Billion-plus people, three million officials, 33 questions - India begins huge census
items.summary: The two-phase exercise - the world’s most ambitious - will see three million officials count every Indian.

items.title: 'A million things could go wrong' - why seizing Iran's uranium would be so risky for the US
items.summary: Seizing the stockpile would be one of the "most complicated special operations in history", a former defence official tells the BBC.

items.title: Asia's migrant workers debate if Gulf jobs are worth deadly risk of Iran war
items.summary: Iran's strikes on Gulf states have been especially hard for migrants who have long supported these economies to lift their families back home from poverty.

items.title: Who are the Artemis II astronauts heading to the Moon?
items.summary: The crew, who are accomplished pilots, engineers and scientists, reveal their hopes, fears and family sacrifices.

items.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
items.summary: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.

items.title: After sex abuse claims, activists and lawmakers rethink Cesar Chavez Day
items.summary: California has renamed Cesar Chavez Day to Farmworkers Day after the labour-rights icon was accused of sexual abuse.

items.title: Shock, sadness and relief in town at centre of Australia's seven-month manhunt for Dezi Freeman
items.summary: Porepunkah has been in the spotlight since Dezi Freeman shot dead two police officers last year.

items.summary: Lunar discoveries and a space race with China is seeing the US invest time and money to get to the Moon - and beyond.

items.summary: After delays and technical issues, the first crewed Moon mission in 50 years finally took off from Florida and is now in Earth's orbit.

items.summary: BBC’s Leyla Khodabakhshi speaks to locals and visitors in Florida ahead of the Artemis II mission blast off.

items.summary: The Artemis II spacesuits are designed to protect the astronauts during launch and landing, as well as possible emergency scenarios.

items.summary: Fugitive Dezi Freeman has been wanted since he shot dead two police officers on his rural property in August last year.

items.title: Hundreds in Beirut mourn journalists killed in Israeli strike
items.summary: BBC reports from the funerals of three journalists killed by a targeted attack in southern Lebanon.
